title: pleopeltis glauca
intro: Pleopeltis glauca, also known as the resurrection fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States, particularly in the subtropical and tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis glauca include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
